senses.definition: Relating to a propyl group attached to acetic acid to form an ester, specifically propyl acetoacetate or similar propyl esters.
examples: Propylacetic ester was used as a building block in the synthesis.
The propylacetic derivative reacts with alcohols in a transesterification.

senses.definition: The plural of propylaeum; a grand gateway or entrance to a sacred or important place, often with classical architecture.
examples: The soldiers stood at the propylaea before entering the temple.
Architects studied the Propylaea for its monumental entrance.

senses.definition: A monumental gateway or vestibule at the entrance of a building or sacred site; the singular form associated with Greek architecture.
examples: The propylaeum leads visitors to the Acropolis.
Researchers described the propylaeum as a ceremonial gateway.

senses.definition: A variant or misspelling related to propylaea/propylaeum; commonly used to refer to a doorway or gateway in classical architecture.
examples: The propylalaea features decorative columns.
An illustrated plate shows the propylalaea at the city gate.

senses.definition: One of two simple amines where a propyl group is attached to a nitrogen atom; a type of chemical base.
examples: Propylamine is used as a building block in organic synthesis.
Propylamine has a fishy odor like other amines.

senses.definition: Plural of propylamine; multiple propylamine molecules or related compounds.
examples: Propylamines are common in organic synthesis.
Different propylamines can be used as catalysts.

senses.definition: The chemical process of introducing a propyl group into a molecule, often to modify its properties.
examples: Propylation changes the compound's reactivity.
The propylation step is key in this synthesis.

senses.definition: A colorless gas or liquid hydrocarbon (C3H6) used to make plastics and chemicals; also called propene.
examples: Propylene is used to make polypropylene.
Propylene oxide is a derivative of propylene.

senses.definition: Plural of propylene; multiple molecules of the propylene substance.
examples: The reactor produced several propylenes this batch.
Impurities in the propylenes affected polymer quality.

senses.definition: A stimulant drug; a propyl derivative of hexedrine used in some pharmaceutical or illicit contexts.
examples: Propylhexedrine is a stimulant found in some decongestants.
Misuse of propylhexedrine can be dangerous.

senses.definition: Relating to or containing the propyl group; used in chemical naming.
examples: The propylic acid is a component of the compound.
Propylic derivatives were synthesized for testing.

senses.definition: A chemical group formed by the connection of a propyl moiety with a double-bonded structure; used in naming alkenes and related compounds.
examples: Propylidene groups help stabilize certain ring systems.
The propylidene intermediate was isolated.

senses.definition: A mineral or mineral-like substance featuring propyl groups; may appear in geological naming contexts or as a typo for related terms.
examples: The sample contained a propylite mineral.
Propylite fragments were noted in the sediment.

senses.definition: Plural form of propylite; mineral-like substances containing propyl groups, or used in geological contexts.
examples: Propylites were examined for composition.
The deposit contained several propylites.

senses.definition: Relating to or resembling propylite or the chemistry of propyl groups; used in mineralogy and geochemistry.
examples: Propylitic alteration forms under specific hydrothermal conditions.
Propylitic rocks show characteristic mineralogy.

senses.definition: The process of introducing propyl groups into a molecule; a chemical reaction pathway.
examples: Propylitisation was employed to modify the compound's properties.
The study explored propylitisation mechanisms.

senses.definition: To add a propyl group to a molecule in a chemical reaction.
examples: Researchers will propylitise the substrate to study changes.
Propylitise the compound under controlled conditions.

senses.definition: Past participle of propylitise; describes a molecule that has had a propyl group added.
examples: The molecule was propylitised to increase solubility.
Propylitised compounds showed altered reactivity.

senses.definition: Third person singular of propylitise; adds a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The catalyst propylitises several substrates.
It propylitises the ring system efficiently.

senses.definition: Present participle of propylitise; the act of adding a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The team is propylitising several intermediates.
Propylitising can modify volatility.

senses.definition: The process of introducing propyl groups into molecules; an alternative spelling of propylation.
examples: Propylitization is common in organic synthesis.
This step involves propylitization to enhance activity.

senses.definition: Alternative form of propylitise; to add a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The chemist attempted to propylitize the substrate.
Propylitize the compound under careful conditions.

senses.definition: Past tense form of propylitize; a molecule that has had a propyl group added.
examples: The compound was propylated to improve solubility.
Propylated derivatives showed different activity.

senses.definition: Third person singular of propylitize; adds a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The method propylitizes several substrates.
Propylitizes the intermediate efficiently.

senses.definition: Present participle of propylitize; the act of adding a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The process is propylitising several intermediates.
Propylitising can modify reactivity.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of propylitisation; the process of introducing propyl groups into molecules.
examples: Propylitization was the focus of the study.
Different routes to propylitization were explored.

senses.definition: Alternative form of propylitise; to add a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: They will propylize the substrate under study.
Propylize the target compound to test effects.

senses.definition: Past participle of propylize; describes a molecule that has had a propyl group added.
examples: The propylitized product showed different properties.
Propylitized compounds were analyzed further.

senses.definition: Third person singular present of propylitize; adds a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The catalyst propylitises several substrates.
It propylitises the ring system efficiently.

senses.definition: Present participle of propylitize; the act of adding a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The process is propylitising several intermediates.
Propylitising can modify reactivity.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of propylitisation or propylation; the process of introducing propyl groups into molecules.
examples: Propylitization was achieved with a new catalyst.
The study compares propylitization methods.

senses.definition: To add a propyl group to a molecule; often used interchangeably with propylitise.
examples: The chemist will propylitize the substrate.
Propylitize the compound under study.

senses.definition: Past participle of propylitize; describes a molecule that has had a propyl group added.
examples: The propylitized product was more soluble.
Propylitized derivatives showed higher activity.

senses.definition: Third person singular present of propylitize; adds a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The process propylitizes several substrates.
It propylitizes the intermediate.

senses.definition: Present participle of propylitize; the act of adding a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: Propylitising increases the compound’s lipophilicity.
The team is propylitising several substrates.

senses.definition: Process of introducing propyl groups into molecules; an alternate spelling of propylitisation/propylation.
examples: Propylitization is used in organic synthesis.
Different routes to propylitization were studied.

senses.definition: Alternative spelling of propylitise; to add a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The chemist will propylize the substrate.
Propylize the compound under study.

senses.definition: Past participle of propylize; describes a molecule that has had a propyl group added.
examples: The propylitized product showed improved properties.
Propylitized compounds were studied further.

senses.definition: Third person singular present of propylitize; adds a propyl group to a molecule.
examples: The catalyst propylitizes several substrates.
It propylitzes the ring system efficiently.

examples: Propylitising alters the compound's reactivity.
